lesson.menu_desc: A guided tour in Paris and getting information -- Can you tell me...? + Is there...? + WH-questions -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 -- Asking About the City -- A Guided Tour & Getting Information
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Gabriela Pires | Aula 16 -- Asking About the City | Alumni by Better

listenings.text: Hello everyone, and welcome to the city tour! My name is Marc, and I am your guide today. Our tour starts here, in front of the most famous landmark in Paris. First, we walk along the river, and then we take a short river cruise. The cruise gives you a wonderful view of the city. You can take photos from the boat. After the cruise, we visit a beautiful old church nearby. The opening hours are from nine in the morning to six in the evening. At the end, there is a small shop where you can buy a souvenir. Please take a brochure from the table; it has a map and all the information. If you have any questions during the tour, just ask me. Now, let us begin our sightseeing. Follow me, and enjoy the view!

Today I went on a guided tour in Paris, and I asked all my questions in English. First, I asked the guide, can you tell me where the tour starts? He showed me the first landmark. Then we took a river cruise, and the view from the boat was amazing. I asked, is there a place to take photos? And he said yes, from the top of the boat. After the cruise, we walked to an old church nearby. I asked about the opening hours, and they were from nine to six. At the end, I bought a small souvenir for my mom, and I took a brochure with a map. The guide was very kind, and I understood almost everything. I went sightseeing and asked for information, all in English!

extra_audio.text: First, you meet the guide and you ask, can you tell me where the tour starts? Next, you walk to the first landmark and you listen to the guide. After that, you take a river cruise and you enjoy the view of the city. Then, you visit an old church nearby and you ask about the opening hours. Finally, you buy a souvenir and you take a brochure with a map.
